title: Inventory Core Math And Timing Modules
summary: Core math and timing module inventory for the vanilla parity rebuild. Captures the nine committed src/core/ TypeScript files (the five runtime math primitives angle.ts, binaryReader.ts, fixed.ts, rng.ts, trig.ts plus the four audit ledgers audit-angle-type-and-wrap-semantics.ts, audit-fixed-divide-semantics.ts, audit-fixed-multiply-overflow-semantics.ts, audit-fixed-point-constants.ts pinned by core-lane steps 04-001/04-002/04-003/04-004), the canonical timing primitive src/host/ticAccumulator.ts owned by the launch lane, and the canonical D_DoomLoop orchestrator src/mainLoop.ts owned by the core lane. Records the size_bytes/line_count/sha256/exports/imports fingerprint of each committed file, the Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 function or table each module models, and the per-module count of src/ runtime importers and test/ importers. Confirms that src/main.ts and src/mainLoop.ts both bypass src/core/ and src/host/ticAccumulator.ts directly today (the simplified launcher reaches src/core/ only transitively through src/launcher/session.ts and src/launcher/win32.ts; src/mainLoop.ts has zero imports), and pins the determinism-boundary status of src/core/ as the bit-exact root every other module depends on for fixed-point math, BAM angles, the LCG-free RNG_TABLE walk, the tantoangle/finesine/finetangent lookups, and the binary-reader cursor.
evidence_method: direct local repository audit performed by the inventory lane: git ls-files src/core/ src/host/ticAccumulator.ts src/mainLoop.ts to enumerate the eleven committed TypeScript files, sha256sum and wc -l over each committed file via createHash and readFileSync inside a Bun script, ripgrep over each module for ^export\s+(?:async\s+)?(?:interface|function|const|class|type)\s+\w+ to enumerate the public surface, ripgrep over each module for ^import\s+.*\sfrom\s+'...' to enumerate external imports, walking every src/**/*.ts and test/**/*.ts file via Bun.Glob and counting basename matches for each target module to derive importer counts, ripgrep over src/main.ts and src/mainLoop.ts for direct imports of src/core/ and src/host/ticAccumulator.ts, and direct read of plan_vanilla_parity/PARALLEL_WORK.md to confirm lane ownership.

timing_module_paths.tic_accumulator_owner_lane_source: plan_vanilla_parity/PARALLEL_WORK.md launch row owns column (covers all of src/host/)
timing_module_paths.main_loop_owner_lane_source: plan_vanilla_parity/PARALLEL_WORK.md core row owns column (explicitly names src/mainLoop.ts)
timing_module_paths.notes: src/host/ticAccumulator.ts is the canonical I_GetTime accumulator (35 Hz tic generation from the high-resolution clock) and is owned by the launch lane because it lives under src/host/. src/mainLoop.ts is the canonical D_DoomLoop orchestrator and is owned by the core lane. Neither module imports any src/core/ math primitive today; the timing primitives are decoupled from the math primitives at the module level even though both are part of the determinism boundary.

modules.vanilla_function_modeled: angle_t typedef plus ANG45/ANG90/ANG180/ANG270 BAM constants from Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 src/tables.h and the implicit unsigned-int wrap semantics that linuxdoom-1.10 src/p_user.c, src/r_main.c, and src/p_mobj.c rely on across angle arithmetic
modules.role: Binary Angle Measurement (BAM) primitive: 32-bit unsigned angle alias, four cardinal-angle constants, and the angleWrap helper that reproduces the C unsigned-int wrap via >>> 0

modules.vanilla_function_modeled: audit ledger pinning the angle_t typedef, the four BAM constants ANG45/ANG90/ANG180/ANG270, the ANG_MAX sentinel, and the natural unsigned-int wrap rule from Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 src/tables.h against the runtime angleWrap export of src/core/angle.ts (the renderer FOV-delta and player hitscan use cases that rely on the wrap behaving exactly like the C unsigned-int operators)
modules.role: core-lane audit ledger committed by step 04-004 audit-angle-type-and-wrap-semantics; exposes contract audit entries, derived invariants, probes, and a BigInt reference angleWrapReference for the focused test under test/vanilla_parity/core/

modules.vanilla_function_modeled: audit ledger pinning the FixedDiv contract from Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 src/m_fixed.c against the runtime fixedDiv export of src/core/fixed.ts (overflow guard (abs(a) >> 14) >= abs(b), sign-XOR clamp to FIXED_MIN/FIXED_MAX, divide-by-zero capture inside the guard, x86 abs(INT_MIN) parity, int64 shift / divide / int32-narrowing on the non-overflow branch)
modules.role: core-lane audit ledger committed by step 04-003 audit-fixed-divide-semantics; exposes contract audit entries, derived invariants, probes, and a BigInt reference fixedDivReference for the focused test under test/vanilla_parity/core/

modules.vanilla_function_modeled: audit ledger pinning the FixedMul int64-multiply / arithmetic-right-shift / int32-narrowing contract from Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 src/m_fixed.c against the runtime fixedMul export of src/core/fixed.ts (the load-bearing two's-complement wrap that demos, fast-projectile physics, AI sound propagation, and the renderer all silently rely on)
modules.role: core-lane audit ledger committed by step 04-002 audit-fixed-multiply-overflow-semantics; exposes contract audit entries, derived invariants, probes, and a BigInt reference fixedMulReference for the focused test under test/vanilla_parity/core/

modules.vanilla_function_modeled: audit ledger pinning FRACBITS, FRACUNIT, FIXED_MIN, and FIXED_MAX against their upstream Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 declarations in src/m_fixed.h and src/doomtype.h (the 16.16 split and the int32 backing type that anchor the entire fixed-point lane)
modules.role: core-lane audit ledger committed by step 04-001 audit-fixed-point-constants; exposes fixed-point constant audit entries, derived invariants, and a cross-check helper for the focused test under test/vanilla_parity/core/

modules.vanilla_function_modeled: cursor-based little-endian byte reader matching the implicit struct-layout reads in Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 src/w_wad.c (lump headers and directory entries), src/p_setup.c (map lump bundles), src/d_main.c (config), src/i_oplmusic.c and src/mus2mid.c (MUS lumps), src/i_sound.c (DMX SFX lump headers), and src/g_game.c (demo lump headers)
modules.role: shared little-endian Buffer cursor used by every WAD parser, demo parser, map-data parser, and sound-lump parser in src/; the only src/core/ runtime module that does not implement a math primitive

modules.vanilla_function_modeled: fixed_t typedef, FRACBITS, FRACUNIT, plus FixedAdd/FixedSub/FixedMul/FixedDiv from Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 src/m_fixed.h and src/m_fixed.c (the 16.16 fixed-point lane every world-coordinate, projection, slope, and AI-step computation in vanilla DOOM rides on)
modules.role: canonical 16.16 fixed-point primitive: type alias, four constants, four arithmetic operators with bit-exact two's-complement and divide-by-zero semantics; the most heavily reused module in the project (46 src/ importers and 62 test importers)

modules.vanilla_function_modeled: rndtable, P_Random, M_Random, P_SubRandom, and M_ClearRandom from Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 src/m_random.h and src/m_random.c (the table-walk PRNG with separate prndindex and rndindex counters that demo playback, monster behavior, hitscan spread, projectile aim, and menu effects all depend on)
modules.role: canonical deterministic LCG-free PRNG: frozen 256-element RNG_TABLE plus the DoomRandom class encapsulating the two index counters and the four canonical operations that keep the gameplay stream synchronized for demo parity

modules.vanilla_function_modeled: finesine[10240], finecosine alias (phase-shifted finesine), finetangent[4096], tantoangle[2049] LUTs and the FINEANGLES, FINEMASK, ANGLETOFINESHIFT, SLOPEBITS, SLOPERANGE, DBITS constants plus the SlopeDiv helper from Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 src/tables.h, src/tables.c, and src/r_main.c (the bit-exact LUT family that the renderer's projection, the sprite/wall column and span pipelines, and the world's angle/movement code rely on)
modules.role: canonical fixed-point trigonometric LUT module: hardcoded sine/cosine/tangent/arctangent tables backed by Int32Array, plus the slopeDiv helper for tantoangle indexing; only src/core/ runtime module that imports a sibling (FRACBITS from src/core/fixed.ts)

modules.vanilla_function_modeled: I_GetTime baseline-anchored 35 Hz tic accumulator from Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 src/i_timer.c (the integer-only delta * 35 / frequency conversion that prevents drift from incremental rounding-error accumulation)
modules.role: canonical timing primitive: TICS_PER_SECOND constant, TicClock interface, TicAccumulator class converting high-resolution bigint clock deltas into discrete tics; consumed by the launcher's win32 message-pump and by every src/playable/real-time-main-loop/ contract module

modules.vanilla_function_modeled: D_DoomLoop main loop orchestrator from Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 src/d_main.c (the four-step pre-loop sequence initialTryRunTics → restoreBuffer → executeSetViewSize → startGameLoop and the four-step per-frame sequence startFrame → tryRunTics → updateSounds → display in the non-singletics, testcontrols=false path)
modules.role: canonical D_DoomLoop orchestrator: MAIN_LOOP_PHASES and PRE_LOOP_STEPS frozen-arrays and counts plus the MainLoop class enforcing strict pre-loop and per-frame call order through MainLoopCallbacks and PreLoopCallbacks

determinism_boundary_status.claude_md_anchor: CLAUDE.md Architecture section labels src/core/ as the determinism boundary: 'src/core/ (fixed.ts, angle.ts, trig.ts, rng.ts, binaryReader.ts) holds the primitives every other module depends on. Fixed-point math (FRACUNIT, fixedMul), the DOOM LCG (DoomRandom), and angle/trig tables must match DOOM bit-exact semantics. Anything that breaks parity here breaks parity everywhere downstream.'
determinism_boundary_status.notes: The five core_math runtime modules form the bit-exact root of the project: every fixed-point coordinate, BAM angle, RNG draw, trig lookup, and binary-cursor read in src/ resolves to one of them. The four audit_ledger modules are not part of the runtime determinism boundary (they have zero src/ importers); they are parity-evidence files committed by core-lane steps 04-001/04-002/04-003/04-004 to pin the runtime exports against their upstream C declarations. The two timing modules complete the determinism boundary on the temporal axis: ticAccumulator turns wall-clock into the canonical 35 Hz tic, and mainLoop orchestrates the canonical D_DoomLoop call order around that tic stream.

vanilla_orchestrator_wiring_status.notes: src/main.ts imports only the five module specifiers ./bootstrap/cmdline.ts, ./launcher/session.ts, ./launcher/win32.ts, ./reference/policy.ts, ./reference/target.ts; it does not directly reference any module under src/core/, src/host/ticAccumulator.ts, or src/mainLoop.ts. The simplified launcher reaches the core math primitives only through src/launcher/session.ts (which imports from src/core/angle.ts, src/core/fixed.ts, src/core/rng.ts) and src/launcher/win32.ts (which imports from src/host/ticAccumulator.ts). src/mainLoop.ts has zero import statements; the canonical D_DoomLoop orchestrator is intentionally pure and wires its callbacks through the MainLoopCallbacks and PreLoopCallbacks interfaces declared in the same file.

implications: src/core/ is owned by the core lane in plan_vanilla_parity/PARALLEL_WORK.md and holds the nine committed modules that are the determinism boundary for vanilla DOOM 1.9 parity; every fixed-point arithmetic operation, BAM angle wrap, RNG draw, and trig lookup in src/ resolves to one of these nine files, so any inventory-lane edit to the directory is forbidden and downstream lanes (gameplay, ai, render, audio, world, map, save) must keep their importers stable when core-lane refactors land.

src/host/ticAccumulator.ts is owned by the launch lane (lives under src/host/) but is the canonical 35 Hz tic generator that every src/playable/real-time-main-loop/ contract module and src/launcher/win32.ts depend on; the launch lane and the core lane must coordinate through DEPENDENCY_GRAPH.md when either reshapes the TicClock interface, the TicAccumulator class, or the TICS_PER_SECOND constant.

src/mainLoop.ts is owned by the core lane and is the canonical D_DoomLoop orchestrator that the launch lane must wire into the bun run doom.ts entrypoint as part of step 03-031 implement-clean-launch-to-title-loop and step 04-014 implement-main-loop-preloop-ordering / 04-015 implement-main-loop-per-frame-ordering; its strict four-pre-loop and four-per-frame call order through MainLoopCallbacks and PreLoopCallbacks must be preserved across every future change.

src/core/fixed.ts is the most heavily reused module in the project (46 src/ importers and 62 test importers) and src/core/rng.ts is the second most reused (17 src/ and 21 test); any rename or reshaping of FRACUNIT, fixedMul, fixedDiv, RNG_TABLE, or DoomRandom is a cross-lane breaking change, so the core lane must coordinate such edits through DEPENDENCY_GRAPH.md before landing them.

The four src/core/audit-*.ts modules have zero src/ importers and exactly one test/ importer each (their matching focused tests under test/vanilla_parity/core/); they are parity-evidence ledgers committed by core-lane steps 04-001/04-002/04-003/04-004 and are not part of the runtime call graph, so downstream readers must not infer that they participate in the determinism boundary at runtime — only the five runtime math modules angle.ts, binaryReader.ts, fixed.ts, rng.ts, and trig.ts plus the two timing modules src/host/ticAccumulator.ts and src/mainLoop.ts do.

src/main.ts and src/mainLoop.ts both bypass src/core/ and src/host/ticAccumulator.ts directly today: the simplified launcher reaches the math and timing primitives only through src/launcher/session.ts and src/launcher/win32.ts, and src/mainLoop.ts has zero imports. The launch lane must extend the bun run doom.ts entrypoint to wire src/mainLoop.ts and src/host/ticAccumulator.ts into the canonical D_DoomLoop startup path when it replaces the simplified launcher, in lockstep with the core lane's tic-accumulator and main-loop-preloop steps under 04-010, 04-011, 04-014, and 04-015.
